purpose: Track conflicting information across official and unofficial specs so the digital twin is built to actual 2019 F-450 parameters, not generic Super Duty specs.

unresolved_conflicts.description: Third-party automotive spec sites often label 4x2 Ford Super Duties as having independent Twin-I-Beam front suspensions. Heavy-duty F-450 fleet models require a solid axle to support high weight ratings.

contradicting_sources.claim: Front Suspension: Twin-I-Beam Independent Coil Spring (4x2)
Front Suspension: Wide-track Monobeam Solid Axle with coil springs (all 4x2 and 4x4 F-450 models)

unresolved_conflicts.mitigation_reconciliation: Authenticity audit voided premature DOCUMENT_SUPPORTED page citations. Public marketing supports general wide-track monobeam architecture (GENERAL_PLATFORM_ARCHITECTURE_SUPPORTED). Exact engineering variant remains unverified until SRC-L1-001 bytes are hashed and inspected. Physical confirmation still required before geometry freeze.

unresolved_conflicts.description: Standard Ford fleet order guides list tires as standard 225/70R19.5G tires, but the actual tire brand (e.g., Continental, Michelin, Goodyear) can vary by factory batch and trim choice.
unresolved_conflicts.mitigation_reconciliation: Factory tire size is modeled generically as 225/70R19.5. Continental Hybrid HS3 is noted as reference-vehicle-specific and must be verified by sidewall inspection.
